epic.arc: The loop's ambient activity stops being something bdo reconstructs from receipts after the fact and becomes a field he watches live. Every node — the gates, the sessions, and the many virtual-node sessions each session spawns — carries state you can see; every pulse between them is a signal carried deterministically and rendered as flow; and every node projects outward to the GitHub work where it is actually happening. Sensed, carried, seen, and clickable: bdo and a managing 'pulse' session perceive the whole circuit and respond to it, instead of judging blind. The system gets a face.
epic.context: The pulse/signal/portal threads of the 2026-06-11 conversation, landed as one arc. The doctrine already makes the log truth and every view a pure fold over it; loop.gaps (done-line 0048) is the embryo of this epic — it composes a session-local view from the global log and hands the session its top gap on entry. This arc grows that one sense-organ into a carried, composed, rendered signal, and pairs it with a seeing surface in the visual language of bdo's own Causality and Living-Loop sketches (the Causality control loop is, literally, orchestrate's heat/cool dynamics drawn by hand). It is the perceiving complement to epic.owner-harness's steering: owner-harness is where bdo stamps; the field is where he and a managing session watch. Monitoring only — it carries and composes, it never judges or stamps (D-4); the one write path stays the pens. Named the sequel to 'all five gates real': the gates now judge for real, so the next question is who senses and conducts them — the worker/manager-node direction (memory: a managing Claude session drives the nodes instead of being the worker).
epic.value: You open one page and see the living system — which nodes are working, what is flowing, what is stuck — and click any node straight through to its real GitHub work, from wherever you are. The portal is the face that makes a fleet of disposable node sessions legible at a glance.
epic.horizon: A flowing, read-only portal in the Causality + Living-Loop language: the node DAG as circles with state-fill, the event stream animated as pulses moving down the edges, the heat/cool control loop alive, each node a projective link to its live GitHub issue or PR, and each session shown with the field of virtual-node circuits it spawned — all one deterministic fold over the log, writing nothing (D-4). When bdo wants to know what the system is doing, he looks at the field, not the log.

pieces.glue: the deterministic signal layer: loop.gaps grown from 'your one top gap' into the carry-and-process fold — node actions (receipts) and live pulses (events) composed into a neighbourhood-scoped, session-local view, so a pulse session has ample information to respond without asking. The data spine every other piece renders; pure read-only fold, the discipline of the log preserved.

the first circuit drawn: the five-gate DAG with per-node state-fill (real / parked / refused), Causality-style, extending loop/web.py (the served fold that already exists) — the thinnest real slice, the proof the field renders from the log.

the Living Loop flow: the events.jsonl stream animated as pulses moving down the edges and nodes lighting as they act — ambient activity made visible, a deterministic replay of the event log, no new state invented.

the outward projection: each node links to its live GitHub ticker — the gate.py trust-rail issue that stays open while a run works, the realness/arc-confirm issues, the PR it lands as — so 'see the node working' is one click to the real surface, never a re-rendering of it.

the field, not just the pipeline: each session and the virtual-node sessions it spawned (merge-node, gate.py minds) drawn from the runs ledger, so one chat session's many nodes read as a visible nodal circuit — bdo's 'field of nodal circuits', the monitoring portal for a fleet of mortal node sessions.
